title: capsella bursa-pastoris var. rubella
intro: Capsella bursa-pastoris var. rubella, also known as red shepherds purse, is a variety of the common weed known as shepherd's purse, which belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of glucosinolates, isothiocyanates, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of red shepherd's purse include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of red shepherd's purse may include infusions, tinctures, or capsules, and should be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
